title: Cenocline aurea
taxonomy: Cenocline aurea is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is a member of the genus Cenocline, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to Central America. The family Polemoniaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the phlox or gilia family. These plants are known for their showy flowers and are often found in dry to moist areas. Cenocline aurea is classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves when it germinates.

intro: Cenocline aurea, also known as the Gold-dust vine or Yellow-flowering Cenocline, is a perennial climber with yellow flowers that belongs to the Bignonaceae family. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, where it has been employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cenocline aurea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
